017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.util.*;
021
022import jakarta.servlet.*;
023import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
024
025/**
026 * Abstract base class for Juneau authentication servlet filters.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Implements the two-layer authentication contract:
030 * <ul>
031 *    <li><b>Chain layer</b> &mdash; subclasses implement {@link #authenticate(HttpServletRequest)}, which returns
032 *       one of three states:
033 *       <ul>
034 *          <li>{@link Optional#empty()} &mdash; filter does not apply (no recognizable credentials in the request).
035 *          <li>{@link Optional#of(Object) Optional.of(AuthResult)} &mdash; credentials recognized and valid; carries
036 *             the resolved {@link java.security.Principal} and role set.
037 *          <li>throw {@link AuthenticationException} &mdash; credentials recognized but invalid (bad token, revoked
038 *             key, etc.).
039 *       </ul>
040 *    <li><b>Standalone filter layer</b> &mdash; {@link #doFilter(ServletRequest, ServletResponse, FilterChain)} is
041 *       provided by this base and calls {@link #authenticate}, wraps the request on success, passes through on
042 *       empty, and sends a {@code 401} response on failure.
043 * </ul>
044 *
045 * <p>
046 * Subclasses are both directly usable as a {@link jakarta.servlet.Filter} (standalone) and composable inside an
047 * {@link AuthFilterChain} (chain mode). In standalone mode, {@link #doFilter} drives the full request lifecycle.
048 * In chain mode, {@link AuthFilterChain} calls {@link #authenticate} directly and handles the response itself.
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
051 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AuthFilterChain}
052 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AuthResult}
053 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AuthenticatedRequestWrapper}
054 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/AuthFilterFramework">AuthN Filter Framework</a>
055 * </ul>
056 *
057 * @since 10.0.0
058 */
059public abstract class AuthFilter implements Filter, Authenticator {
060
061   /** WWW-Authenticate response header name (RFC 7235 §4.1). */
062   static final String WWW_AUTHENTICATE = "WWW-Authenticate";
063
064   /**
065    * Standalone-filter entry point.
066    *
067    * <p>
068    * Calls {@link #authenticate}, and on success wraps the request in an {@link AuthenticatedRequestWrapper}
069    * and delegates to the next filter.  On {@link AuthenticationException}, writes a {@code 401} response with
070    * the {@code WWW-Authenticate} challenge from the exception.  On an empty result the request passes through
071    * unchanged.
072    *
073    * <p>
074    * When this filter is composed inside an {@link AuthFilterChain}, the chain calls
075    * {@link #authenticate(HttpServletRequest)} directly and this method is not invoked.
076    */
077   @Override /* Overridden from Filter */
078   public void doFilter(ServletRequest req, ServletResponse resp, FilterChain chain)
079         throws IOException, ServletException {
080      var hreq = (HttpServletRequest) req;
081      var hresp = (HttpServletResponse) resp;
082      try {
083         var result = authenticate(hreq);
084         if (result.isPresent()) {
085            chain.doFilter(new AuthenticatedRequestWrapper(hreq, result.get()), resp);
086         } else {
087            chain.doFilter(req, resp);
088         }
089      } catch (AuthenticationException e) {
090         sendChallenge(hresp, e);
091      }
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * No-op default implementation.
096    */
097   @Override /* Overridden from Filter */
098   public void init(FilterConfig cfg) throws ServletException {
099      // No-op
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * No-op default implementation.
104    */
105   @Override /* Overridden from Filter */
106   public void destroy() {
107      // No-op
108   }
109
110   /**
111    * Writes a 401 response with the {@code WWW-Authenticate} challenge from the exception.
112    *
113    * @param resp The HTTP response.
114    * @param e The authentication failure.
115    * @throws IOException If writing to the response fails.
116    */
117   @SuppressWarnings({
118      "resource" // Writer lifecycle is servlet-managed; do not close.
119   })
120   public static void sendChallenge(HttpServletResponse resp, AuthenticationException e) throws IOException {
121      e.getHeaders().stream()
122         .filter(h -> WWW_AUTHENTICATE.equalsIgnoreCase(h.getName()))
123         .map(h -> h.getValue())
124         .findFirst()
125         .ifPresent(v -> resp.setHeader(WWW_AUTHENTICATE, v));
126      resp.setStatus(HttpServletResponse.SC_UNAUTHORIZED);
127      var msg = e.getMessage();
128      if (msg != null)
129         resp.getWriter().write(msg);
130   }
131}
